chunks.text: Yun S. Song is a professor of EECS and Statistics working in mathematical and computational biology. His honors and awards include NIH Pathway to Independence Award K99/R00 (2006), Alfred P. Sloan Research Fellowship (2008), Packard Fellowship for Science and Engineering (2008), NSF CAREER Award (2009), Jim and Donna Gray Faculty Award for Excellence in Undergraduate Teaching (2013), Miller Research Professorship (2014), Math+X Simons Chair (2015), and Chan Zuckerberg Biohub Investigator Award (2017).
chunks.question: What is Yun S. Song's academic focus and notable awards?
chunks.answer: Mathematical and computational biology with multiple prestigious awards.

chunks.text: Education: 2001, PhD, Physics, Stanford University; 1997, B.S., Mathematics, MIT; 1996, B.S., Physics, MIT. Research Areas: Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), Artificial Intelligence (AI), Theory (THY), Applied Probability and Statistics. Research Centers: Center for Computational Biology (CCB), Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing (SITC).
chunks.question: What are Yun S. Song's educational background and research areas?
chunks.answer: PhD from Stanford, BS from MIT in computational biology and AI.

chunks.text: Teaching Schedule: CS 70. Discrete Mathematics and Probability Theory, TuTh 12:30-13:59, Dwinelle 155. Selected Publications include works on DNA language models predicting genome-wide variant effects, cross-protein transfer learning for disease variant prediction, CherryML for phylogenetic models, translation efficiency parameters, protein transfer learning evaluation, population-specific recombination maps, tensor decomposition for gene expression, likelihood-free inference in population genetics, coalescence time analysis, ribosomal interference impact, operator norm inequalities, population history inference, deep learning for genetic inference, population size history inference, demographic inference limits, spectral methods for selection, Descartes' rule in demographics, genealogical property distortion, Padé approximants, and diffusion process transition densities.
chunks.question: What does Yun S. Song teach and what are his research publications about?
chunks.answer: Teaches CS 70 and publishes on computational biology and genetics.

chunks.text: Contact Information: 304B Stanley Hall, tel: 510-642-2351, yss@berkeley.edu. Office Hours: M 1-2pm, Th 5:30-6:30pm, 629 Soda. Links: Personal Homepage, Dissertations, Research Support. Catie Anders: cmanders@berkeley.edu. Selected Honors and Awards: Jim and Donna Gray Award for Excellence in Undergraduate Teaching of Computer Science (2013), NSF Faculty Early Career Development Award (CAREER) (2009), Sloan Research Fellow (2008), Packard Fellow in Science and Engineering (2008).
chunks.question: How can one contact Yun S. Song and what are some of his honors?
chunks.answer: Contact via email and office hours; has teaching and research awards.
